reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Gate 1 (Dead Code): ComputeForkDigest has production callers in bootnode.go:221. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Attack path uses P2P bootnode discovery (untrusted entry point). Gate 3 (Code Verification): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct—ComputeForkDigest (signing_root.go:304-310) uses only fork_version + genesis_validators_root without XOR, while entryWithForkDigest (config.go:576-590) correctly applies blob parameter XOR for Fulu epochs. Bootnode.go:221 directly calls the non-XOR version. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this by running a bootnode with same ForkVersion but different MaxBlobsPerBlock, causing identical digests for distinct networks. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-027 in outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json (lines 730-747) states 'Fork digest must differ when blob parameters change, even if fork version is unchanged (EIP-7892)'. Gate 6 (Scope): Fusaka-specific (EIP-7892), P2P network, in scope. Severity: Prysm has 31% CL market share, below the 33% threshold for High severity ('network splits affecting more than 33%') but well above 5% for Medium. Classified as CONFIRMED_POTENTIAL rather than CONFIRMED_VULNERABILITY because the attack requires the attacker to operate bootnode infrastructure and for victims to discover and connect to the malicious bootnode, making the practical exploitability uncertain compared to direct message-based attacks.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Fork digest must differ when blob parameters change, even if fork version is unchanged (EIP-7892). Assertion: forall e1,e2: blob_params(e1)!=blob_params(e2) => fork_digest(e1)!=fork_digest(e2)
